title: hieracium murorum subsp. gentile
intro: Hieracium murorum subsp. gentile, also known as gentile wall hawkweed, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the dandelion family (Asteraceae). The plant is primarily used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Hieracium murorum subsp. gentile include its potential to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of digestive issues. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in the form of infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, and is traditionally consumed as a tea or applied topically as a salve or ointment.
